blocks.content: They cut through the alley behind the dollar store, where the pavement was wide enough to ride side by side.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I looked it up. It means we legally cannot go in there.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Okay as in you understand, or okay as in you're already planning to anyway?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The sign makes it more interesting, not less.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: It means the building falls on you.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I hate that word so much.
Kit grinned. The alley leveled out and the sun hit them all at once as they came out onto Main Street.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Yeah but now it's hot and I have to look at it.
The Tasty Freeze was at the far end of the block, lawn chairs already out in the parking lot.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Half the town's always there in June.
Daisy May Jones was sitting on the low brick wall in front of the pharmacy with Savannah Brooks, eating something red out of a paper cup. She hadn't looked up yet.
He did not appear, in any visible way, to care.
He did, however, stop messing with his hair, because it was already fine.
